core_insight: Legal users expect AI answers to ground in primary legislation rather than secondary sources, especially for transaction-heavy topics like real estate purchase contracts.
Legal users want exhaustive enumeration of statutory mandatory elements (e.g., for heat supply contracts) rather than partial answers.
Legal users want an Improve Prompt feature that suggests a more precise, structured version of their query (with jurisdiction, legal basis, role) before submission to raise output quality.
citation: It would be highly beneficial to introduce an "Improve Prompt" feature that helps users refine their input before sending it to the model.

core_insight: Legal users want to upload 50–100 files (or scale by subscription tier) so that workflows like M&A due diligence, complex litigation and contract portfolio analysis can be handled without repeated uploads.
citation: The current limitation on the number of uploaded attachments (e.g. 15 files) is restrictive for real legal workflows.
